mnemonics.title: System Testing Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: System testing is like a puzzle, where all the pieces must fit together perfectly.

mnemonics.title: Black Box Testing Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Black box testing is like trying to solve a mystery box without knowing what's inside.

mnemonics.title: White Box Testing Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: White box testing is like having the blueprint to a house, where you can see all the inner workings.

mnemonics.title: Gray Box Testing Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: Gray box testing is like having a map to a treasure chest, where you have some idea of what's inside.

acronyms.usage: System testing is a crucial phase in the software development life cycle.
Black box testing is a technique where the tester has no knowledge of the system's internal workings.
White box testing is a technique where the tester has knowledge of the system's internal workings.
Gray box testing is a technique where the tester has some knowledge of the system's internal workings.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a puzzle with many pieces that must fit together perfectly.
Imagine a mystery box with unknown contents.
Imagine a blueprint to a house with all the inner workings visible.
Imagine a map to a treasure chest with some idea of what's inside.

analogies.analogy: A puzzle with many pieces that must fit together perfectly.
Trying to solve a mystery box without knowing what's inside.
Having the blueprint to a house with all the inner workings visible.
Having a map to a treasure chest with some idea of what's inside.
